entries.content: Michael promised a 'live music experience' for last year's office party that turned out to be his iPod through a Bluetooth speaker - need to manage expectations and budget clarity for future events
Michael responds better to multiple curated options with guardrails rather than open-ended suggestions for party planning decisions
Dwight is very effective at party setup logistics but should not be given authority over security matters
User has had a negative interaction with Angela that they felt was mean-spirited
Ryan tends to talk excessively about strategy and industry trends, often during work time, causing noticeable reactions (eye-rolls) from sales team and Jim
Pam agreed to monitor Ryan's talking pattern and gather concrete examples before escalating to Michael as a formal operational issue

Michael's 'live music experience' event last year had issues with inadequate planning and advance notice to the team; Pam learned that locking in event details before promising to staff prevents stress and ensures better outcomes
User is interested in planning a London office party; may want to revisit this once a realistic timeline is established
User is planning a summer office party and wants to secure headcount confirmation, potential dates (July or August), and budget from accounting before finalizing details
User has learned from previous year's party experience and wants to avoid repeating past mistakes
